one_time_description: Contingent liability of $4,391,870 in loans to Wei Wai Kum Kwiakah Treaty Society from Indigenous Services Canada was forgiven by the Federal Government of Canada as of March 31, 2020
economic_notes: Nation owns a townhouse in Campbell River BC acquired during fiscal year 2020 rented to related family member of Chief and Council. Nation also owns a condominium rented to another related family member. Saaiyouck Development Corporation investigates and pursues business opportunities within traditional territories. A-Tlegay Fisheries Limited Partnership acquires and manages fishing licences and quota. Related party 0986442 BC Ltd. trades in crypto currency.
notes: Nation has two GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: A-Tlegay Fisheries Limited Partnership (12.99% ownership) and Saaiyouck Development Corporation (100% ownership). Budget figures were all zero/not provided in the statement. Loan of $30,000 advanced to related party 0986442 BC Ltd. (crypto currency company). Deferred revenue of $175,000 remaining from London Enterprises Ltd. $250,000 payment received March 2017 for land access recognized over 10 years to March 2027. COVID-19 noted as subsequent event likely to have material impact on fiscal 2021. Contingent liability of $4,391,870 treaty loan forgiven as of March 31, 2020 - text appears cut off in note 10. total_government_transfers includes ISC $378,712 + Kwakiutl District Council $5,467 + Province of BC $20,195 = $404,374.
